name: Gravlax (Salmon Marinated in Dill)
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Fresh salmon fillet, center-cut, cleaned and scaled
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the salmon in half lengthwise and remove the backbone and small bones. Leave the skin on. Place half of the fish, skin side down, in a deep glass, enamel, or stainless steel baking dish or casserole.
2. Wash and shake dry the bunch of dill. Scatter it on the fish. If the herbs are not very pungent, chop them coarsely and sprinkle over the fish instead.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the salt, sugar, and crushed peppercorns. Sprinkle this mixture evenly over the dill.
4. Top with the other half of the fish, skin side up. Cover with foil and set a heavy plate or platter slightly larger than the salmon on top, pressing down to ensure contact.
5. Refrigerate for at least 3 days, up to 7 days. Turn the fish over every 12 hours, basting it with the liquid marinade that accumulates. Replace the platter and weights each time.
6. When ready, remove the fish from the marinade and scrape away the dill and seasonings. Pat dry with paper towels. You may choose to leave the dill and seasonings in place.
7. Place the separated halves skin side down on a carving board and slice the salmon thinly against the grain. Gravlax is served as part of a smorgasbord or appetizer, usually accompanied by a mustard-dill sauce (see recipe) and lemon wedges if served as a main course with toast and perhaps a cucumber salad.
